sql: SELECT id, name, department, salary, CASE WHEN salary >= #{highSalary} THEN 'VIP' WHEN salary >= #{midSalary} THEN 'Regular' ELSE 'Basic' END as tier, CASE WHEN salary >= (SELECT AVG(salary) FROM users WHERE department = u.department) THEN 'AboveAvg' ELSE 'BelowAvg' END as salary_comparison FROM users u WHERE u.status IN (#{statusList}) ORDER BY #{sortBy}
templateSql:
SELECT
    id, name, department, salary,
    CASE
        <when test="tierField == 'salary'">
            WHEN salary >= #{highSalary} THEN 'VIP'
            WHEN salary >= #{midSalary} THEN 'Regular'
            ELSE 'Basic'
        </when>
        <when test="tierField == 'department'">
            WHEN department = 'Sales' THEN 'SalesTeam'
            WHEN department = 'Tech' THEN 'TechTeam'
            WHEN department = 'HR' THEN 'HRTeam'
            ELSE 'Other'
        </when>
        <otherwise>
            WHEN status = 'active' AND department = 'Tech' THEN 'ActiveTech'
            WHEN status = 'active' THEN 'ActiveOther'
            ELSE 'Inactive'
        </otherwise>
    END as tier,
    CASE
        WHEN salary >= (SELECT AVG(salary) FROM users WHERE department = u.department)
        THEN 'AboveAvg'
        ELSE 'BelowAvg'
    END as salary_comparison,
    CASE
        <foreach collection="customRules" item="rule">
            WHEN ${rule.condition} THEN #{rule.result}
        </foreach>
        ELSE 'default'
    END as custom_tier
FROM users u
<where>
    <if test="statusList != null and statusList.size() > 0">
        AND u.status IN
        <foreach collection="statusList" item="s" open="(" separator="," close=")">
            #{s}
        </foreach>
    </if>
</where>
<if test="sortBy != null">
    ORDER BY ${sortBy}
</if>
